methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $15.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.5B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Applied Intuition, a Series F AI & Software company, is evaluated against a universe of nine AI & Software comparables: one public (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private peers including Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, and Skild AI. The company carries a last post-money valuation of $15.0B following $600M+ in total capital raised.

Two triangulation methods yield material divergence. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.3x multiplier typical of Series F advancement, implying a valuation of $19.5B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Crusoe Energy at $10.0B, SandboxAQ at $5.8B, Ayar Labs at $3.8B, and Lambda at $2.5B) yields a median of $4.8B, with a range of $2.5B to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneous market conditions and difficulty in isolating true comparability within the frontier AI sector.

Triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $4.0B to $22.4B, with a median of $12.1B. The current $15.0B valuation sits above the median but below the stage-progression upside case, reflecting typical Series F pricing between peer median and next-round step-up expectations. Key uncertainties include: revenue generation relative to peer cohorts, market timing for a potential exit window, and whether frontier AI company multiples will compress or expand given macroeconomic and competitive dynamics in the sector.
